Flowering time in-situ

Justine Floret

2024-07-02

Library and Data preparation

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(reshape2) #melt function
library(forcats) #reorganise in geom boxplot
library(kableExtra) # Markdown table

# Stat library 
# library(lme4)
library(lmerTest) #glht
# library(MASS)
library(lsmeans) #pairwise comparison
library(multcomp)  #glht
library(multcompView)  #glht
library(rstatix) # manova levene test
library(emmeans)

# Graphic library
library(GGally) #ppair plot
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(svglite) # save in svg format

setwd("/home/justine/sciebo - Floret, Justine (jfloret@uni-koeln.de)@uni-koeln.sciebo.de/1_Populations/4_workflows/April_2024/")

data_all <- read_csv("/home/justine/sciebo - Floret, Justine (jfloret@uni-koeln.de)@uni-koeln.sciebo.de/1_Populations/2_assay/dataset/Flowering_time_1.csv")

data_all$Site_ID <- as.factor(data_all$Site_ID)
data_all$Area <- as.factor(data_all$Area)
data_all$Type <- as.factor(data_all$Type)
data_all$Destroyed <- as.factor(data_all$Destroyed)
data_all$Sowing_1st <- as.factor(data_all$Sowing_1st)
data_all$Sowing_2nd <- as.factor(data_all$Sowing_2nd)
data_all$Presence_April_22 <- as.factor(data_all$Presence_April_22)
data_all$Presence_April_23 <- as.factor(data_all$Presence_April_23)
data_all$Presence_April_24 <- as.factor(data_all$Presence_April_24)
data_all$Persistence <- as.factor(data_all$Persistence_1)

Formating the date

Removing the destroyed site until 04/2023 the date of the measurement

data_all_ND <- data_all %>% dplyr::filter(Destroyed == "Exist" | Destroyed == "04/2024"| Destroyed == "04/2023" )
table(data_all_ND$Type)
## 
##    grass pavement Tree bed     wall 
##      118      139      105       93
data_Ath <- data_all_ND %>% dplyr::filter(Presence_April_23 == "Yes")
data_Ath$Type <- as.factor(data_Ath$Type)

Formatting flowering time date : “late” FT is transformed in 30/04/23 to show a late FT

data_Ath <- data_Ath %>% mutate(First_FT = replace(First_FT, First_FT == "late", "04/30/23"))
data_Ath <- data_Ath %>% mutate(First_FT = replace(First_FT, First_FT == "11/22", "11/01/22"))
data_Ath$First_FT  <- as.Date(data_Ath$First_FT, "%m/%d/%y")

data_Ath <- data_Ath %>% mutate(`100%_FT` = replace(`100%_FT`, `100%_FT` == "late", "04/30/23"))
data_Ath <- data_Ath %>% mutate(`100%_FT` = replace(`100%_FT`, `100%_FT` == "11/22", "11/01/22"))
data_Ath$`100%_FT`  <- as.Date(data_Ath$`100%_FT`, "%m/%d/%y")

Julian days

data_Ath$First_FT_julianday  <- as.POSIXlt(data_Ath$First_FT)$yday 
data_Ath$First_FT_julianday  <-as.numeric(data_Ath$First_FT_julianday)

data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday`  <- as.POSIXlt(data_Ath$`100%_FT`)$yday 
data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday`  <-as.numeric(data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday`)

Transform the value of 11/22 to -61 to count from 01/01/23 and go backward in time

data_Ath <- data_Ath %>% mutate(`First_FT_julianday` = replace(`First_FT_julianday`, `First_FT_julianday` == "304", "-61"))
data_Ath <- data_Ath %>% mutate(`100_FT_julianday` = replace(`100_FT_julianday`, `100_FT_julianday` == "304", "-61"))

Flowering time variation between the first flower and 100% population flowering in number of days

data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday` <- as.numeric(data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday`)
data_Ath$`First_FT_julianday` <- as.numeric(data_Ath$`First_FT_julianday`)

data_Ath$FT_variation <- data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday` - data_Ath$First_FT_julianday +1
#write_csv(data_Ath, "/home/justine/sciebo - Floret, Justine (jfloret@uni-koeln.de)@uni-koeln.sciebo.de/1_Populations/2_assay/dataset/Flowering_time_1.csv")

Merged the data with FT with the complete data set

data_all <- data_all[,1:21]
data_all <- left_join(data_all, data_Ath[,c(1,48,49,50)], by = "Site_ID")

#write_csv(data_all, "/home/justine/sciebo/1_Populations/2_assay/dataset/Flowering_time_final.csv")

First Flower

all datas

Include sowing Sep. 2021 and July 2022.
Since there is only one Tree bed site in the second sowing I am removing the population

data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Sowing_1st == "07/2022") %>% dplyr::filter(Type == "Tree bed") 
## # A tibble: 1 × 50
##   Site_ID Area      Localization_ID Type     Destroyed Outliers_soil Sowing_1st
##   <fct>   <fct>     <chr>           <fct>    <fct>     <chr>         <fct>     
## 1 457     Zollstock 196             Tree bed Exist     Not sampled   07/2022   
## # ℹ 43 more variables: Sowing_2nd <fct>, Germ_2ndyear <chr>,
## #   Presence_April_22 <fct>, Presence_April_23 <fct>, Presence_April_24 <fct>,
## #   Persistence_1 <dbl>, Persistence_2 <dbl>, Feb_22 <dbl>, April_22 <dbl>,
## #   October_22 <dbl>, November_22 <dbl>, April_23 <dbl>, `FT-04-23` <dbl>,
## #   Cohort <chr>, First_FT <date>, `50%_FT` <chr>, `100%_FT` <date>,
## #   `11/22` <dbl>, `03/01/23` <dbl>, `03/11/23` <dbl>, `03/13/23` <dbl>,
## #   `03/15/23` <dbl>, `03/16/23` <chr>, `03/17/23` <dbl>, `03/18/23` <dbl>, …
data_Ath <- data_Ath[data_Ath$Site_ID != "457", ]
data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday` <- as.numeric(data_Ath$`100_FT_julianday`)
data_Ath$`First_FT_julianday` <- as.numeric(data_Ath$`First_FT_julianday`)

Graphics :

colors <- c("#6ece58","#22a884", "#355f8d","#440154")
data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(First_FT_julianday >40) %>%
  mutate(Type = fct_relevel(Type, 
            "grass", "Tree bed", "pavement", "wall")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Type, y=First_FT_julianday, fill=Type)) + geom_boxplot() + geom_jitter(width = 0.2) + 
  ylim(50,120) + theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) 

#ggsave("FT_Sowing21-22", plot = last_plot(),dpi = 900, device = "svg", bg = "white")
#ggsave("/home/justine/sciebo/Paper_urban_niche/Figures/FT_Sowing21-22.svg", plot = last_plot(),dpi = 900, device = "svg", bg = "transparent")
colors <- c("#6ece58","#22a884", "#355f8d","#440154")
data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(First_FT_julianday >40) %>%
  mutate(Type = fct_relevel(Type, 
            "grass", "Tree bed", "pavement", "wall")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Type, y=First_FT_julianday, fill=Type, alpha = Sowing_1st)) + geom_boxplot()  + 
  geom_jitter(position=position_jitterdodge()) +
  ylim(50,120) + theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  scale_alpha_discrete(range = c(0.5,1))

#ggsave("FT_Sowing21-22", plot = last_plot(),dpi = 900, device = "svg", bg = "white")
#ggsave("/home/justine/sciebo/Paper_urban_niche/Figures/FT_Sowing21-22.svg", plot = last_plot(),dpi = 900, device = "svg", bg = "transparent")
data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Type=="wall") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=First_FT_julianday)) + geom_histogram(fill="#440154")

data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Type=="grass") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=First_FT_julianday)) + geom_histogram(fill="#6ece58")

data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Type=="pavement") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=First_FT_julianday)) + geom_histogram(fill="#22a884")

data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Type=="Tree bed") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=First_FT_julianday)) + geom_histogram(fill="#355f8d")

Models :

The Poisson law doesn’t allow negative value, they are transformed into 0

data_Ath_mod <- data_Ath %>% mutate(`First_FT_julianday` = replace(`First_FT_julianday`, `First_FT_julianday` == -61, 0))
data_Ath_mod <- data_Ath_mod %>% mutate(`100_FT_julianday` = replace(`100_FT_julianday`, `100_FT_julianday` ==  -61, 0))
data_Ath_mod$Sowing_1st <- as.factor(data_Ath_mod$Sowing_1st)

Negative binomial model

mod12 <- MASS::glm.nb(First_FT_julianday ~ Type*Sowing_1st, data = data_Ath_mod)

mod13 <- glm(First_FT_julianday ~ Type*Sowing_1st, data = data_Ath_mod ,family=negative.binomial(theta=mod12[["theta"]]))
summary(mod13)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = First_FT_julianday ~ Type * Sowing_1st, family = negative.binomial(theta = mod12[["theta"]]), 
##     data = data_Ath_mod)
## 
## Coefficients: (1 not defined because of singularities)
##                                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                     4.378270   0.070286  62.293   <2e-16 ***
## Typepavement                   -0.066411   0.086992  -0.763    0.446    
## TypeTree bed                    0.021160   0.057840   0.366    0.715    
## Typewall                       -0.006876   0.078791  -0.087    0.931    
## Sowing_1st09/2021              -0.003268   0.083169  -0.039    0.969    
## Typepavement:Sowing_1st09/2021  0.049358   0.101705   0.485    0.628    
## TypeTree bed:Sowing_1st09/2021        NA         NA      NA       NA    
## Typewall:Sowing_1st09/2021      0.071580   0.099666   0.718    0.473    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(14.479) family taken to be 0.6053064)
## 
##     Null deviance: 296.56  on 218  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 293.76  on 212  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 2050.4
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
car::Anova(mod13)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: First_FT_julianday
##                 LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)
## Type              3.2899  3     0.3490
## Sowing_1st        1.6514  1     0.1988
## Type:Sowing_1st   0.5177  2     0.7719
qqnorm(resid(mod13))

Poisson model

mod14 <- glm(First_FT_julianday ~ Type*Sowing_1st, data = data_Ath_mod ,family=poisson())
summary(mod14)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = First_FT_julianday ~ Type * Sowing_1st, family = poisson(), 
##     data = data_Ath_mod)
## 
## Coefficients: (1 not defined because of singularities)
##                                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                     4.378270   0.035422 123.604   <2e-16 ***
## Typepavement                   -0.066411   0.044277  -1.500    0.134    
## TypeTree bed                    0.021160   0.029084   0.728    0.467    
## Typewall                       -0.006876   0.039732  -0.173    0.863    
## Sowing_1st09/2021              -0.003268   0.041931  -0.078    0.938    
## Typepavement:Sowing_1st09/2021  0.049358   0.051677   0.955    0.340    
## TypeTree bed:Sowing_1st09/2021        NA         NA      NA       NA    
## Typewall:Sowing_1st09/2021      0.071580   0.050036   1.431    0.153    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1171.1  on 218  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 1152.8  on 212  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 2504.1
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
car::Anova(mod14)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: First_FT_julianday
##                 LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## Type             12.9939  3    0.00465 **
## Sowing_1st        6.5591  1    0.01043 * 
## Type:Sowing_1st   2.0363  2    0.36125   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
qqnorm(resid(mod14))

pairs(emmeans (mod14,  ~ Type | Sowing_1st))
## Sowing_1st = 07/2022:
##  contrast            estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  grass - pavement     0.06641 0.0443 Inf   1.500  0.2909
##  grass - Tree bed      nonEst     NA  NA      NA      NA
##  grass - wall         0.00688 0.0397 Inf   0.173  0.9836
##  pavement - Tree bed   nonEst     NA  NA      NA      NA
##  pavement - wall     -0.05954 0.0321 Inf  -1.855  0.1519
##  Tree bed - wall       nonEst     NA  NA      NA      NA
## 
## Sowing_1st = 09/2021:
##  contrast            estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  grass - pavement     0.01705 0.0266 Inf   0.640  0.9191
##  grass - Tree bed    -0.02116 0.0291 Inf  -0.728  0.8861
##  grass - wall        -0.06470 0.0304 Inf  -2.128  0.1443
##  pavement - Tree bed -0.03821 0.0234 Inf  -1.631  0.3610
##  pavement - wall     -0.08176 0.0251 Inf  -3.263  0.0061
##  Tree bed - wall     -0.04354 0.0276 Inf  -1.576  0.3926
## 
## Results are given on the log (not the response) scale. 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for varying family sizes
pairs(emmeans (mod14, ~ Sowing_1st | Type))
## Type = grass:
##  contrast              estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  (07/2022) - (09/2021)  0.00327 0.0419 Inf   0.078  0.9379
## 
## Type = pavement:
##  contrast              estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  (07/2022) - (09/2021) -0.04609 0.0302 Inf  -1.526  0.1270
## 
## Type = Tree bed:
##  contrast              estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  (07/2022) - (09/2021)   nonEst     NA  NA      NA      NA
## 
## Type = wall:
##  contrast              estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  (07/2022) - (09/2021) -0.06831 0.0273 Inf  -2.502  0.0123
## 
## Results are given on the log (not the response) scale.

sowing batch 2021

data_Ath_21 <- data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Sowing_1st == "09/2021") %>% dplyr::filter(Sowing_2nd == "no")

data_Ath_21 %>% dplyr::filter(First_FT_julianday >40) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Type, y=First_FT_julianday, fill=Type)) + geom_boxplot() + geom_jitter(width = 0.2) + 
  ylim(50,120) + theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) 

#ggsave("FT_Sowing21", plot = last_plot(),dpi = 900, device = "svg", bg = "white")

Models :

The Poisson law doesn’t allow negative value, they are transformed into 0

data_Ath_21_mod <- data_Ath_21 %>% mutate(`First_FT_julianday` = replace(`First_FT_julianday`, `First_FT_julianday` == -61, 0))
data_Ath_21_mod <- data_Ath_21_mod %>% mutate(`100_FT_julianday` = replace(`100_FT_julianday`, `100_FT_julianday` ==  -61, 0))

Poisson 

mod14 <- glm(First_FT_julianday ~ Type, data = data_Ath_21_mod ,family=poisson())
summary(mod14)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = First_FT_julianday ~ Type, family = poisson(), 
##     data = data_Ath_21_mod)
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   4.37500    0.02244 194.970   <2e-16 ***
## Typepavement -0.01607    0.02796  -0.575   0.5655    
## TypeTree bed -0.01705    0.03028  -0.563   0.5732    
## Typewall      0.06470    0.03041   2.128   0.0334 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 963.75  on 129  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 952.61  on 126  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 1746.4
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
qqnorm(resid(mod14))

car::Anova(mod14)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: First_FT_julianday
##      LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)  
## Type   11.135  3    0.01102 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
car::Anova(mod14, test.statistic="F")
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: First_FT_julianday
## Error estimate based on Pearson residuals 
## 
##           Sum Sq  Df F value Pr(>F)
## Type       11.13   3  0.7251 0.5388
## Residuals 644.99 126
summary(glht(mod14 , mcp(Type="Tukey")))
## 
##   Simultaneous Tests for General Linear Hypotheses
## 
## Multiple Comparisons of Means: Tukey Contrasts
## 
## 
## Fit: glm(formula = First_FT_julianday ~ Type, family = poisson(), 
##     data = data_Ath_21_mod)
## 
## Linear Hypotheses:
##                            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## pavement - grass == 0    -0.0160660  0.0279573  -0.575   0.9394  
## Tree bed - grass == 0    -0.0170533  0.0302750  -0.563   0.9426  
## wall - grass == 0         0.0647037  0.0304126   2.128   0.1433  
## Tree bed - pavement == 0 -0.0009872  0.0262895  -0.038   1.0000  
## wall - pavement == 0      0.0807697  0.0264479   3.054   0.0118 *
## wall - Tree bed == 0      0.0817570  0.0288870   2.830   0.0238 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## (Adjusted p values reported -- single-step method)

sowing bacth 2022, only new site

Environment Tree bed is remove, only one sites for July 2022 sowing

data_Ath_22 <- data_Ath %>% dplyr::filter(Sowing_1st == "07/2022")
data_Ath_22 <- data_Ath_22 %>% dplyr::filter(Type == "grass" | Type == "pavement" |Type == "wall")
colors <- c("#6ece58", "#355f8d", "#440154")
ggplot(data_Ath_22, aes(x = Type, y=First_FT_julianday, fill=Type)) + geom_boxplot() + 
  theme_minimal() + scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  geom_jitter(shape=16, position=position_jitter(0.2), size = 2)

Models :

mod14 <- glm(First_FT_julianday ~ Type, data = data_Ath_22 ,family=poisson())
summary(mod14)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = First_FT_julianday ~ Type, family = poisson(), 
##     data = data_Ath_22)
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   4.378270   0.035422 123.604   <2e-16 ***
## Typepavement -0.066411   0.044277  -1.500    0.134    
## Typewall     -0.006876   0.039732  -0.173    0.863    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 159.57  on 67  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 155.65  on 65  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 581.97
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
qqnorm(resid(mod14))

car::Anova(mod14)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: First_FT_julianday
##      LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)
## Type   3.9221  2     0.1407

Population is 100% flowering

Sowing batch 2021

colors <- c("#6ece58", "#22a884","#355f8d","#440154")

data_Ath_21 %>% dplyr::filter(First_FT_julianday >40) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Type, y=`100_FT_julianday`, fill=Type)) + geom_boxplot()  + 
  theme_minimal() + scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  geom_jitter(shape=16, position=position_jitter(0.2), size = 2)

mod14 <- glm(`100_FT_julianday` ~ Type, data = data_Ath_21_mod,family=poisson())
summary(mod14)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = `100_FT_julianday` ~ Type, family = poisson(), 
##     data = data_Ath_21_mod)
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   4.55135    0.02055 221.525  < 2e-16 ***
## Typepavement  0.03429    0.02537   1.351 0.176618    
## TypeTree bed  0.05704    0.02727   2.092 0.036457 *  
## Typewall      0.10431    0.02760   3.780 0.000157 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 398.87  on 129  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 383.06  on 126  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 1221.8
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
qqnorm(resid(mod14))

car::Anova(mod14)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: 100_FT_julianday
##      LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## Type    15.81  3    0.00124 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(glht(mod14 , mcp(Type="Tukey")))
## 
##   Simultaneous Tests for General Linear Hypotheses
## 
## Multiple Comparisons of Means: Tukey Contrasts
## 
## 
## Fit: glm(formula = `100_FT_julianday` ~ Type, family = poisson(), 
##     data = data_Ath_21_mod)
## 
## Linear Hypotheses:
##                          Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## pavement - grass == 0     0.03429    0.02537   1.351   0.5286    
## Tree bed - grass == 0     0.05704    0.02727   2.092   0.1547    
## wall - grass == 0         0.10431    0.02760   3.780   <0.001 ***
## Tree bed - pavement == 0  0.02276    0.02331   0.976   0.7618    
## wall - pavement == 0      0.07003    0.02369   2.956   0.0164 *  
## wall - Tree bed == 0      0.04727    0.02571   1.838   0.2540    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## (Adjusted p values reported -- single-step method)

Variation to have the all population at 100% FT

Sowing batch 2021

ggplot(data_Ath_21, aes(x = Type, y=FT_variation, fill=Type)) + geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() + scale_fill_viridis(discrete = TRUE, begin = 0.3, end = 0.9, option = "B" ) + scale_alpha_discrete(range = c(0.5,1))+ 
  geom_jitter(shape=16, position=position_jitter(0.2), size = 2)

data_Ath_21 %>% dplyr::filter(First_FT_julianday >40) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Type, y=FT_variation, fill=Type)) + geom_boxplot()  + 
  theme_minimal() + scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  geom_jitter(shape=16, position=position_jitter(0.2), size = 2)

Model

mod14 <- glm(FT_variation ~ Type, data = data_Ath_21_mod ,family=poisson())
summary(mod14)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = FT_variation ~ Type, family = poisson(), data = data_Ath_21_mod)
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   2.79239    0.04951  56.404  < 2e-16 ***
## Typepavement  0.24694    0.05909   4.179 2.93e-05 ***
## TypeTree bed  0.43420    0.06109   7.108 1.18e-12 ***
## Typewall      0.45743    0.06194   7.386 1.52e-13 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 2714.3  on 129  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 2640.4  on 126  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 3207
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 6
qqnorm(resid(mod14))

car::Anova(mod14)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: FT_variation
##      LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## Type   73.863  3  6.351e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(glht(mod14 , mcp(Type="Tukey")))
## 
##   Simultaneous Tests for General Linear Hypotheses
## 
## Multiple Comparisons of Means: Tukey Contrasts
## 
## 
## Fit: glm(formula = FT_variation ~ Type, family = poisson(), data = data_Ath_21_mod)
## 
## Linear Hypotheses:
##                          Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## pavement - grass == 0     0.24694    0.05909   4.179   <0.001 ***
## Tree bed - grass == 0     0.43420    0.06109   7.108   <0.001 ***
## wall - grass == 0         0.45743    0.06194   7.386   <0.001 ***
## Tree bed - pavement == 0  0.18725    0.04818   3.887   <0.001 ***
## wall - pavement == 0      0.21049    0.04925   4.274   <0.001 ***
## wall - Tree bed == 0      0.02323    0.05163   0.450    0.969    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## (Adjusted p values reported -- single-step method)
leastsquare = lsmeans(mod14, pairwise ~ Type,adjust = "tukey")
cld(leastsquare)
##  Type     lsmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL .group
##  grass      2.79 0.0495 Inf      2.70      2.89  1    
##  pavement   3.04 0.0323 Inf      2.98      3.10   2   
##  Tree bed   3.23 0.0358 Inf      3.16      3.30    3  
##  wall       3.25 0.0372 Inf      3.18      3.32    3  
## 
## Results are given on the log (not the response) scale. 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 4 estimates 
## significance level used: alpha = 0.05 
## NOTE: If two or more means share the same grouping symbol,
##       then we cannot show them to be different.
##       But we also did not show them to be the same.
